questions.question: Implement the Boolean function F(A,B,C) = Σm(1,3,5,6) using only NAND gates. Explain why NAND gates are called universal gates and demonstrate how any Boolean function can be realized using only NAND gates.
keyPoints: Express function in sum-of-minterms form and derive simplified expression using Boolean algebra or K-map
Show circuit implementation using only NAND gates through double complementation technique
NAND is universal because AND, OR, NOT operations can all be derived using only NAND gates
Any combinational circuit can be designed using only NAND gates (functional completeness)

questions.question: Using a 4-variable Karnaugh map, simplify the Boolean expression F(A,B,C,D) = Σm(0,2,5,7,8,10,13,15). Show all groupings (including essential prime implicants) and derive the minimal SOP expression. Verify the result using Boolean algebra.
keyPoints: Construct 4-variable K-map with correct cell adjacencies (including wrap-around)
Group adjacent 1s in powers of 2 (groups of 8, 4, 2, 1) to maximize simplification
Identify essential prime implicants by checking if 1 is covered by only one group
Derive simplified expression by eliminating variables that change within groups

questions.question: (a) Design a 4-to-1 multiplexer using basic gates (AND, OR, NOT). Show the complete circuit diagram with select lines S1 and S0, and explain its operation with the truth table. (b) Implement the function F(A,B,C) = Σm(1,3,5,7) using a 4-to-1 multiplexer with A and B as select lines. (c) Explain how multiplexers are used in data routing and as universal logic generators.
keyPoints: 4-to-1 MUX: Use 4 data inputs, 2 select lines, 1 output; implement using 4 AND gates (each with one input AND select lines), 1 OR gate to combine AND outputs
For F(A,B,C) with A,B as select: connect C, C', 0, 1 to data inputs based on minterm expansion
Multiplexers can implement any Boolean function by connecting variables to select lines and appropriate values to data inputs
Applications: data selection, parallel-to-serial conversion, function generation, routing control signals

questions.question: Design a full adder circuit that adds three input bits (A, B, and Cin) and produces Sum and Carry_out. (a) Derive the Boolean expressions for Sum and Carry_out using Boolean algebra and K-maps. (b) Implement the full adder using only NAND gates (show circuit diagram). (c) Draw the block diagram of two full adders connected in cascade to add two 2-bit numbers, and explain the propagation of carries. (d) Compare the implementation of half adder and full adder, highlighting why full adder is essential for multi-bit addition.
keyPoints: Sum = A ⊕ B ⊕ Cin (Σm(1,2,4,7)), Carry_out = AB + ACin + BCin (Σm(3,5,6,7)); derive using K-maps for two-level minimization
NAND gate implementation: use De Morgan's theorems and double complementation to convert AND-OR to NAND-NAND form
Cascaded adders: Connect Sum outputs to result bits, Carry_out of first adder to Carry_in of second adder (ripple carry)
Half adder lacks Cin input making it unsuitable for multi-bit addition; full adder handles carry propagation essential for binary arithmetic
